title: EVgo Plans Over 14,000 DC Fast Charging Stalls by 2029
text: EVgo revealed an updated expansion plan for its DC fast-charging network with an ambitious target of deploying up to 14,400 stalls by the end of 2029. The company ended 2024 with approximately 4,080 stalls in operation and added more than 1,230 new DC fast charging stalls during the year. For 2025, the company planned to deploy 800-850 stalls with more recent guidance indicating higher targets of 1,400-1,650 new stalls including public, dedicated, and eXtend stalls. The public network is projected to reach 4,000 stalls by the end of 2025 and exceed 5,000 by the end of 2026, with close to 10,000 stalls available publicly by the end of 2028. On December 12, 2024, EVgo closed a loan guarantee of up to $1.25 billion from the US Department of Energy Loan Programs Office to build approximately 7,500 fast charging stalls across the US over the next five years. This DOE funding significantly supports aggressive deployment targets through 2029.
